era_context: Early Bronze Age III; Old Kingdom Egypt in decline; Early Dynastic III Mesopotamia; Indus Valley Civilization at maturity. This period is characterized by the persistence of established urban centers and trade networks, with the first signs of stress in the Egyptian Old Kingdom system.

events.title: Pepi II's reign — Old Kingdom Egypt
events.description: Pepi II continues to reign. This year falls within the period when the centralized authority of the Old Kingdom is gradually weakening, with provincial governors (nomarchs) accumulating more power and resources. Archaeological evidence suggests that while the state apparatus continued to function, the concentration of wealth and authority in the hands of the pharaoh was diminishing.
events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for this year. Dating is conventional.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record for this year is sparse. Specific events are not documented; the year is significant only as part of the broader trend of Old Kingdom decline. The surviving record is biased toward monumental inscriptions and tomb texts, which may not reflect the full complexity of political and economic changes occurring.
